title: Preparation and cryopreservation of human whole blood samples for analysis by flow cytometry (fresh or after cryobanking)
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"6m0ah","text":"This protocol focuses on the preparation of human whole blood samples for analysis by flow cytometry. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":44,"length":26}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"djuhh","text":"It provides two options for sample preparation: (1) immediate flow cytometry with fresh blood samples or (2) cryopreservation of the samples at -80°C using the Stable-Lyse/Store V2 system from SmartTube Inc. for flow cytometry at a later timepoint.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":82,"length":6},{"style":"bold","offset":109,"length":17},{"style":"bold","offset":160,"length":20}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"4mi7o","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"2ot5b","text":"Note: This protocol does not provide guidelines on how to create a multicolor flow cytometry panel!","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":4}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
guidelines: {"blocks":[{"key":"j5sr","text":"This protocol is only tested on EDTA anti-coagulated whole blood. ","type":"ordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":32,"length":32}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"9v2qr","text":"Experiments for whole blood samples are performed at room temperature. Nevertheless, if you cannot process your sample directly after collection, keep the collection tubes at 4 °C until processing, especially if you want to store the plasma for later analysis (however, extended storage periods, ie. several hours, might influence frequency and phenotype of specific immune cell types, especially myeloid cells).","type":"ordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":16,"length":20},{"style":"bold","offset":53,"length":16},{"style":"bold","offset":175,"length":4},{"style":"bold","offset":234,"length":6}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"2jj1v","text":"Work with filter tips!","type":"ordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":10,"length":11}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"mnvn","text":"When working with fluorochrome-conjugated antibodies, avoid bright (sun) light and consider using aluminum foil to protect your samples from light while incubating.","type":"ordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":18,"length":34}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"45iqd","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"2k0ab","text":"Advantages:","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":10}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"9a82v","text":"Analyze all major immune cell subtypes including granulocytes with flow cytometry.","type":"ordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":49,"length":13},{"style":"bold","offset":67,"length":14}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"c1aoj","text":"No pre-selection/exclusion of certain immune cell subpopulations (e.g. like when isolating PBMC). ","type":"ordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":26}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"49b6","text":"The procedure itself is easier and faster, as it does not require densitiy-gradient centrifugation.","type":"ordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":24,"length":17}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"aigl4","text":"Ready-to-use quality-controlled cryo-preservation buffers.","type":"ordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":12}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"3u9mg","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"anf6g","text":"Limitations:","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":12}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"8ql2e","text":"As mentioned above, this protocol has only been tested on EDTA-anticoagulated blood. ","type":"ordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":58,"length":25}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"f96ck","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"70rff","text":"Qualifications:","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":15}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"88ns9","text":"We recommend basic experience in wet-lab work (e.g. how to pipette) to handle this protocol.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":13,"length":32}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"899pk","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
before_start: {"blocks":[{"key":"c8502","text":"Make sure you read the safety warnings regarding untested human samples!","type":"ordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":49,"length":22}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"cjmct","text":"Make sure an ethics approval according to your institution´s guidelines has been obtained before working with subjects or patient samples!","type":"ordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":13,"length":15}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"3or7p","text":"Revisit the materials list to make sure all the equipment, materials and reagents are available to you.","type":"ordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":12,"length":15}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
warning: {"blocks":[{"key":"fu2u","text":"If you work with untested human samples (e.g. status of Hepatitis B/C and HIV is unknown), all experiments must be carried out in a Class II biosafety cabinet (with laminar air flow). Liquid waste has to be collected and autoclaved before disposal, contaminated materials have to be collected and disposed of separately from other lab waste as they are potentially infectious.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":17,"length":23},{"style":"bold","offset":45,"length":1},{"style":"bold","offset":132,"length":50},{"style":"bold","offset":221,"length":10},{"style":"bold","offset":296,"length":23}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"5ivoe","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"9a2vq","text":"Use caps/lids to close your FACS tube when removing them from the biosafety cabinet for centrifugation. steps: Depending on whether you want to perform flow cytometry on fresh or cryo-preserved samples, you can select different protocol options at this step.

Label your 5 ml FACS tubes . You might need several tubes per sample, depending on the number of panels and controls you are planning to run. GOOD TO KNOW - FMO (fluorescence minus one) controls are helpful to discriminate positive and negative (unspecific background fluorescence) signals, especially if the positive cell populations are not distinctly separated from the negative population. In an FMO tube, all fluorochromes in the panel are present except the fluorochrome in question.

Prepare the antibody mix : For each FACS tube, use 20 µL blocking buffer and the appropriate amount of each of your antibodies . Always prepare the master mix for one extra sample or add 10% for pipetting errors. EXAMPLE 1 2 blood samples (one full stain and one control each) + 1 extra for pipetting error = 5x mix 20 antibodies in panel (1µl needed per sample) Blocking Buffer: 5 x 20 µl = 100 µl 5 µl per antibody final volume of antibody master mix: 200 µl mix to add per FACS tube = 20 µl blocking buffer + 20 µl antibodies = 40 µl EXAMPLE 2 2 blood samples (one full stain and one control each) = 4x mix +10% 20 antibodies in panel (1µl needed per sample) Blocking Buffer: 4x 20 µl = 80 µl (+ 10%) = 88 µl Each antibody: 4 µl per antibody +10% = 4,4 µl per antibody final volume of antibody master mix: 176 µl mix to add per FACS tube = 20 µl blocking buffer + 20 µl antibodies = 40 µl
Invert the EDTA blood collection tube several times to homogenize, especially if the samples have been kept in the fridge.
Transfer 200 µL of whole blood to each of your prelabeled 5 ml FACS tubes.

Add the appropriate concentration of fixable viability stain directly to the whole blood in each of the FACS tubes and mix well by flicking the tube or vortexing. HOW TO "FLICK" - Mix well by holding the upper part of the tube between thumb and index finger of one hand and at the same time, gently flick the bottom of the tube repeatedly with the index finger of the other hand.
Incubate for 00:05:00 at Room temperature .

Add the appropriate amount of the prepared antibody mix (see Step 3) to each tube. Gently pipette up and down to mix. EXPERT TIP - In case you are using FMO control(s), you can prepare the antibody mix for all your tubes without the FMO antibody(ies) and add them later to your full stain tube.
Incubate 00:20:00 at Room temperature .
Add 2 mL 1X Lysis Buffer to each FACS tube and mix well by pipetting up and down. HOW TO - Works best using a 1000 µl pipet
Incubate for 00:10:00 at Room temperature .
Add 2 mL FACS buffer and mix well by pipetting up and down.
Spin 500 x g, Room temperature, 5 minutes

Discard the supernatant by decanting and resuspend the cell pellet in the remaining buffer by flicking the tube. HOW TO DECANT - Empty the supernatant with momentum and - while keeping the FACS tube in an upside-down position to not disrupt the pellet on the bottom - dip the tube onto a paper towel to get rid of the excess liquid. Turn the FACS tube upright and gently flick to resuspend.
Add 1 mL 1X Lysis Buffer and mix well by pipetting up and down.
Incubate 00:05:00 at Room temperature .
Add 2 mL FACS buffer and mix well by pipetting up and down.
Spin 500 x g, Room temperature, 5 minutes
Discard the supernatant and resuspend the cell pellet in the remaining buffer by flicking the tube.
Add 1 mL Fixation Buffer and mix well by pipetting up and down.
Incubate for 00:10:00 at Room temperature .
Add 2 mL FACS buffer and mix well by pipetting up and down. After fixation, samples can be further processed on the bench, a biosafety cabinet is no longer necessary.
Spin 500 x g, Room temperature, 5 minutes
Discard the supernatant and resuspend the cell pellet in the remaining buffer by flicking the tube.

Add 200 µL FACS buffer and proceed to sample aquisition. Fixed samples can be stored at 4 °C overnight and recorded the next day. However, keep in mind that fixatives such as formalin can change the autofluorescence of your cells and destabilize fluorochromes, especially tandem dyes. If possible, record you samples directly after fixation.
Invert the EDTA blood collection tube several times to homogenize, especially if the samples have been kept in the fridge.

If you do not need to include a fixable viability stain, add 250 µL whole blood to each of your 1,8 ml cryotubes and directely proceed with go to step # GOOD TO KNOW - Keep in mind that it is not possible to add a viability stain at a later timepoint in the protocol - after fixation, all cells will stain positive.
In case you want to use a fixable viability stain, add 250 µL whole blood to each of your pre-labeled 5 ml FACS tubes .
Add 350 µL Stable-Lyse Buffer to each cryotube containing whole blood and mix well by pipetting up and down.
Incubate 00:15:00 at Room temperature .
Add 1000 µL Stable-Store Buffer to each cryotube and mix well by pipetting up and down.
Close the cryotubes and incubate 00:15:00 at Room temperature . After fixation, samples can be further processed on the bench, a biosafety cabinet is no longer necessary.
Immediately transfer to -80 °C for long-term storage. Samples can be stored for several months.

Add the appropriate amount of fixable viability stain directly to the whole blood samples and mix well by flicking the tube. (If you are including an unstained control , do NOT add fixable viability stain to this tube). HOW TO "FLICK" - Mix well by holding the upper part of the tube between thumb and index finger of one hand and at the same time, gently flicking the bottom of the tube repeatedly with the index finger of the other hand.
Incubate 00:05:00 at Room temperature .

After incubation, add 2 mL FACS buffer to each tube and mix well by pipetting up and down. GOOD TO KNOW - After incubation, the remaining fixable viability stain should be diluted and washed off. If you leave it in your sample, it will bind to your fixed cells and more or less all cells will stain positive. HOW TO MIX - Works best using a 1000 µl pipet
Spin 500 x g, Room temperature, 5 minutes .
Using a 1000 µl pipet, carefully pipette off 2 mL of the supernatant without disrupting the cells (including erythrocytes) on the bottom of the tubes.
Gently mix the remaining volume (it should be approximately 250 µL ) and transfer to the corresponding 1,8 ml cryotube .
Add 2 mL FACS buffer to each tube and mix well by pipetting up and down.
Spin 500 x g, Room temperature, 5 minutes .

Discard the supernatant by decanting and resuspend the cell pellet in the remaining buffer by flicking the tube. HOW TO DECANT - Empty the supernatant with momentum and - while keeping the FACS tube in an upside-down position to not disrupt the pellet on the bottom - dip the tube onto a paper towel to get rid of the excess liquid. Turn the FACS tube upright and gently flick to resuspend.
go to step #3 and repeat wash-spin-discard supernatant (steps 13-15). Afterwards, follow-up with step 16 .

In the meantime, prepare the antibody mix : For each FACS tube, use 20 µL blocking buffer and the appropriate amount of each of your antibodies . Count 1 extra sample or 10% for pipetting errors. EXAMPLE 1 2 blood samples (one full stain and one control each) + 1 extra for pipetting error = 5x mix 20 antibodies in panel (1µl needed per sample) Blocking Buffer: 5 x 20 µl = 100 µl 5 µl per antibody final volume of antibody master mix: 200 µl mix to add per FACS tube = 20 µl blocking buffer + 20 µl antibodies = 40 µl EXAMPLE 2 2 blood samples (one full stain and one control each) = 4x mix +10% 20 antibodies in panel (1µl needed per sample) Blocking Buffer: 4x 20 µl = 80 µl (+ 10%) = 88 µl Each antibody: 4 µl per antibody +10% = 4,4 µl per antibody final volume of antibody master mix: 176 µl mix to add per FACS tube = 20 µl blocking buffer + 20 µl antibodies = 40 µl
Transfer your samples from -80 °C to an open rack in the fridge ( 4 °C ) and let them thaw for approximately 01:00:00 .

Add the appropriate amount of the prepared antibody mix to each tube. Gently pipette up and down to mix. In case you are using FMO control(s), you can prepare the antibody mix for all your tubes without the FMO antibody(ies) and add them later to your full stain tube.
Incubate 00:20:00 at Room temperature .
Add 2 mL FACS buffer to each tube.
Spin 500 x g, Room temperature, 5 minutes
Discard the supernatant and resuspend the cell pellet in the remaining buffer by flicking the tube.
Add 200 µL FACS buffer and proceed to sample aquisition.
Transfer the thawed samples to their corresponding 5 ml FACS tubes .

Collect peripheral blood by venipuncture directly into a collection tube with EDTA and invert several times to mix and ensure proper anti-coagulation Blood samples should be processed as soon as possible. If immediate processing is not possible, keep samples at 4°C.

Label 5 ml FACS tubes and 1,8 ml cryotubes to store your samples. Remember to prepare additional aliquots to allow for staining controls at analysis (such as FMO or unstained control ). GOOD TO KNOW - Long-term fixation and storage might considerably change the autofluorescence spectra of your cells of interest. Therefore, it is recommended to include at least one extra aliquot for an unstained control to optimize the quality of your flow cytometry data if needed. add step for venipunture here!!
